definition: The total value of goods a country makes.
example: Like counting all the snacks made in a bakery.
Like candy becoming more expensive.
Like swapping snacks.
Like selling lemonade to another class.
Like buying cookies from another class.
Like tokens used in games.

definition: How much one currency equals another.
example: Like trading 2 candies for 1 chocolate.

definition: When a country produces more over time.
example: Like a garden growing bigger.
Like fewer snacks being made in the bakery.
Like how many apples are in the basket.
Like everyone wanting the same toy.

definition: Prices decided by buyers and sellers.
example: Like students deciding snack prices.
Like banning someone from trading cards.

definition: Putting money into something to grow it.
example: Like planting seeds to grow plants.
Like many kids trading toys.

definition: People who work in an economy.
example: Like students helping in a school project.

definition: How much work is done efficiently.
example: Like finishing homework quickly.
Like some kids having many toys and others few.
Like trading more toys than you receive.
Like the school store running out of money.
